title: abutilon hulseanum
intro: Abutilon hulseanum, also known as the Chinese or Indian mallow, is a plant species in the mallow family Malvaceae. It is traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic properties, which are attributed to the presence of saponins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. Abutilon hulseanum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, although its effectiveness and safety are not extensively documented in modern scientific literature.
